title: Hanseong Group in Talks to Acquire Swedish AI Firm for $800M
body: Seoul-based Hanseong Group is in advanced negotiations to acquire Vantara AI, the European analytics company, for approximately 800 million U.S. dollars, according to a person briefed on the discussions. The deal would give Hanseong, which operates retail, logistics, and semiconductor businesses, a foothold in AI-driven consumer data tools for its European distribution subsidiaries. Ji-Yeon Kwon, the group's chief financial officer, has led the negotiations on Hanseong's side, the person said. A deal could be announced as early as next month, though talks could still fall apart. Hanseong declined to comment. Vantara AI also declined to comment. The reported valuation represents a premium of roughly 35 percent over Vantara's current market capitalization. The acquisition would be one of the largest Korean purchases of a European technology company in recent years. Some analysts questioned the timing, given the ongoing regulatory scrutiny Vantara faces in Ireland.
